fact: Vasco da Gama landed here in 1498 — opening Europe's sea route to India! City of Spices & the famous Zamorin kings. 🐔Kozhi(Chicken)+🧥Coat = Kozhikode
Gateway to Kerala via the Palakkad Gap in the Western Ghats. Famous for the ancient Palakkad Fort built by Hyder Ali. 🥛Paal(Milk)+🌲Kadu(Forest) = Palakkad
Kerala's only district fully in the Western Ghats! Home to Edakkal Caves with carvings from 6000 BCE. 🌾Vayal(Paddy field)+🗺️Nadu(Land) = Wayanad
Home to Asia's largest arch dam! The narrow valleys here are so tight that the name literally means 'narrow place'. 🤏Idukku(Narrow/Pinch)+🔑Key = Idukki
First fully literate district in India! Also called Land of Letters, Latex & Lakes — rubber and backwaters define it. 🏰Kotta(Fort)+🏠Akam(Inside) = Kottayam
Famous for the mystical Theyyam ritual — a living tradition 2000 years old where performers become gods. 👁️Kannu(Eye)+🚪Ur/Door(Town) = Kannur
Home to Kottakkal Arya Vaidya Sala, a 100+ year old Ayurvedic institution known worldwide. ⛰️Mala(Hill)+🏯Puram(Fort/City) = Malappuram
A historic port town called the 'Mecca of Kerala' for its grand Juma Masjid, one of the oldest mosques in Asia. 🪙Pon(Gold)+🐘Aana/Aani(Elephant) = Ponnani
The Venice of the East! The Nehru Trophy Boat Race here has been held since 1952. Houseboats are a way of life. 🌊Ala(Wave)+🛶Puzha(River) = Alappuzha
Cultural capital of Kerala! The Thrissur Pooram — 100 decorated elephants and thundering percussion — is unmatched anywhere. 3️⃣Thri(Three)+⛪Soor(Divine) = Thrissur
A sweet little town on the Periyar riverbank! The name literally sounds like 'Halwa', the beloved Indian sweet. 🍮 sounds like Halwa → Aluva
A misty paradise at 1600m — home to the world's highest tea gardens! Three rivers (Moonu Aar) meet here. 3️⃣Moonu(Three)+⛰️Aar(River/Peak) = Munnar
Pilgrimage capital of Kerala and gateway to Sabarimala! Millions of devotees pass through each year. 🥣Pathram(Vessel)+🧱Thitta(Platform) = Pathanamthitta
The Guruvayur Sri Krishna Temple is one of India's most visited temples! Elephants here get named after kings of old. 👨‍🏫Guru(Teacher)+🌬️Vayu(Wind God) = Guruvayur
A breathtaking backwater village on Vembanad Lake! Bird sanctuary here is a paradise for migratory birds. 👦Kumaran(Boy/Young man)+🏘️Kom(Village) = Kumarakom
Cashew capital of the world! Over 70% of India's cashew is processed here. Also known as Quilon, one of Kerala's oldest ports. ⚡Kol(Power/pole)+🏠Am(place/home) = Kollam
A cliff-beach town famous for its natural mineral springs — the water here has therapeutic properties! 🪨Varku(Rock/cliff)+🌊Ala(Wave) = Varkala
An iconic crescent-shaped beach near Thiruvananthapuram known as the Lighthouse Beach. Kerala tourism was born here! 🥥Kochu(Small)+🌊Alam(Grove/Land) = Kovalam
Birthplace of the famous Thalassery Biryani AND Kerala circus! Circus arts were developed here in the 1800s. 🎂Thalas(sweet/cake)+🌊Sery(place near sea) = Thalassery
Northernmost district of Kerala with 7 languages spoken! Home to Bekal Fort — the largest fort in Kerala. 🏰Kasara(Fort)+🐄Godu(cattle) = Kasargod
India's financial capital! The city never sleeps — and it handles 70% of India's maritime trade. Named after Mumbadevi, the patron goddess.
Capital of India with 3000+ years of history! It has been destroyed and rebuilt 8 times. More trees per km² than any major Indian city.
Silicon Valley of India! The name means 'town of boiled beans' (Benda Kalu Ooru). Home to 40% of India's IT companies.
India's first railway station opened here in 1853! Marina Beach is the world's longest urban beach at 13km.
City of Joy! The Howrah Bridge was built WITHOUT a single nut or bolt — held together purely by rivets. Weighs 26,500 tonnes.
Pearl City of India! Hyderabadi Biryani has a 400-year-old recipe. The Charminar was built in 1591 to ward off plague.
The Pink City! Buildings were all painted terracotta pink in 1876 to welcome Prince Albert of Wales. The colour has stuck since.
One of the world's oldest continuously inhabited cities — over 3000 years! Mark Twain called it 'older than history, older than tradition'.
Home to the Golden Temple (Harmandir Sahib) — the holiest Sikh shrine! The temple serves free food (langar) to 100,000 people daily.
India's smallest state by area! Has more Christian churches per sq km than anywhere in Asia — built during 450 years of Portuguese rule.
Released in 1975, Sholay ran continuously for 5 YEARS in a Mumbai cinema! 'Kitne aadmi the?' became one of cinema's most quoted lines.

answer_alts: dilwale dulhania le jayenge|dilwale dulhaniya le jayenge
fact: Ran for 25 consecutive years at Mumbai's Maratha Mandir! Shah Rukh Khan improvised the iconic outstretched arm scene himself.
The film that made millions question their career choices! Became Bollywood's highest-grossing film at the time of release (2009).
Based on the true story of Mahavir Singh Phogat's daughters winning gold at the Commonwealth Games. Aamir Khan gained 25kg for the role!
Oscar-nominated (2001)! About Indian villagers who challenge British officers to cricket to avoid paying oppressive taxes.
One of the most expensive Indian films of 1960 — took 15 years to make! The iconic 'Pyaar Kiya To Darna Kya' scene used 150,000 mirrors.
The film that changed how Bollywood portrays male friendship! Farhan Akhtar's directorial debut. Shot in Goa and Sydney.
Kangana Ranaut won the National Award for Best Actress! A woman travels to Paris and Amsterdam alone for her honeymoon after her fiancé dumps her.
Karan Johar's debut film (1998) swept 8 Filmfare Awards! SRK wore a skirt-like lungi throughout the college scenes — considered very bold then.
Shot entirely in Spain! Title means 'You won't get life again'. The skydiving, deep-sea diving & tomato-fight scenes were all real.
James Cameron drew all of Jack's sketches himself! The film's production budget ($200M) exceeded the cost of building the actual Titanic.
Based on Shakespeare's Hamlet! 'Hakuna Matata' means 'No Worries' in Swahili. The film's wildebeest stampede took 3 years to animate.
J.K. Rowling wrote the first book idea on a train delay! The entire series has sold over 500 million copies — more than the Bible in the same period.
Christopher Nolan wrote the script over 10 years! The spinning top at the end was NOT Cobb's totem — his wedding ring was. Watch for it!
Heath Ledger won a posthumous Oscar for the Joker. He stayed IN CHARACTER between takes and kept a method-acting diary for the role.
Tom Hanks earned $65M through profit participation! The feather CGI used in opening/closing scenes was considered revolutionary in 1994.
The T-Rex's iconic roar was crafted from a baby elephant's cry, a tiger's snarl, an alligator's hiss, and a penguin's squawk!
Macaulay Culkin was only 10 years old! It held the record for highest-grossing comedy film for 27 years. The tarantula was real.
'Nemo' means 'no one' in Latin! Pixar animators got scuba-certified to research marine life. The film made clownfish sales spike 40%.
Became the highest-grossing film EVER, surpassing Avatar! The 'Portals' scene was filmed over 11 days with 1000+ extras.
Brought to India by Persian traders! The word comes from 'Birian' (fried before cooking). Each Indian city has its own distinct style.
A 2000-year-old South Indian breakfast! The fermentation of rice and lentils makes it probiotic and one of the healthiest fast foods on earth.
Originally from Central Asia! Called 'Sanbusak' in Arab cookbooks from the 10th century. It arrived in India via Persian traders in the 13th century.
Invented by ACCIDENT in 1947 at Moti Mahal Delhi! A chef mixed leftover tandoori chicken into a tomato-butter sauce. Now it's India's most exported dish.
Called Gol Gappa in Delhi, Puchka in Kolkata, Pani Puri in Mumbai! Said to have originated in ancient Magadha (modern Bihar) 2000+ years ago.
Called the world's most perfect breakfast! The 12-hour fermentation makes it rich in probiotics. Ancient Tamil texts from 920 CE mention it.
A favourite Delhi breakfast! Bhature dough is fermented overnight — the puffiness comes from the trapped steam when deep-fried.
Kolkata and Odisha have been in a decades-long legal battle over its origin! Odisha finally won the GI tag in 2019. Both are delicious though.
Invented in 1850s Mumbai to feed textile mill workers a quick hot meal! The 'mashing' technique was borrowed from Portuguese cooking.
A Kerala breakfast with a spongy centre and lacy crispy edges! The secret is fermented rice batter + coconut milk. Pairs perfectly with stew.
